_comment_2: Captured against current HEAD build output. Prior baseline preserved as _previous_baseline below for delta tracking.
_comment_4: Update this fixture with a justification in the commit message when intentional bundle growth lands. Spec 11 fails if any non-runtime-editor chunk grows by more than 5%.
_comment_5: Runtime-editor chunks (RuntimeEditorShell, TenantUserPicker, RuntimeHostTestPage, TenantRouteTree) are excluded — they're EXPECTED to change as R-2+ phases land.
_comment_6: R-2.1 growth: 10 entity-card-section sub-component files (DeliveryCard 4 + AncillaryCard 3 + OrderCard 3) + entity-card-sections registrations shim + InspectorPanel outer-tabs strip + Selection union + selectSection action increases the main bundle by ~22.5 KB (5,544,572 → 5,567,053 bytes, +0.41%). Well under the +0.5% target. RuntimeEditorShell chunk grew 25.13 KB → 30.66 KB (+22%) excluded per policy.
_comment_7: Baseline refreshed 2026-05-13. Drift accumulated from R-2.1 baseline (5,567,053 bytes captured 2026-05-08): Arcs 4a / 4a.1 / 4a.2a / 4b.1a / 4b.1b / 4b.2a / 4b.2b / 4c / 4d landed cumulative ~377kB growth across the May 8-13 ship window. Studio 1a-i.A1 added ~43kB shell substrate. Studio 1a-i.A2's eager-import of RuntimeEditorShell briefly added 111kB; restored to lazy boundary by the 2026-05-13 test maintenance commit (a9d26f0). Current baseline reflects the lazy-boundary post-state at HEAD.

main_bundle.description: Tenant + admin app entry — combined ChunkA bundle that all routes depend on. 2026-05-13 refresh: +7.54% over R-2.1 baseline absorbing Arc 4 family (4a/4a.1/4a.2a/4b.1a/4b.1b/4b.2a/4b.2b/4c/4d) + Studio 1a-i shell substrate. RuntimeEditorShell lazy boundary restored — eager-import drift from A2 fully recovered.

css.description: Compiled Tailwind v4 + DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md token surface. 2026-05-13 refresh: +0.97% over R-2.1 (additional classes from Arc 4 components + Studio shell). Well under tolerance.

chart_renderer.description: Lazy-loaded recharts wrapper for SavedView chart variants. Unchanged since R-2.1.
